computed.formula: CASE WHEN risk_score<=4 THEN 'low' WHEN risk_score<=9 THEN 'medium' WHEN risk_score<=16 THEN 'high' ELSE 'critical' END

modules.name: RiskRegister
modules.description: Complete risk register with list and heatmap views

modules.name: MyRisks
modules.description: Risks and mitigation actions assigned to current user

GET /api/risks/analytics/by-category.description: Risk count by category and level

events.name: risk:created
events.description: Notify risk managers of new high/critical risk

events.name: risk:escalated
events.description: Notify stakeholders when risk is escalated

events.name: risk:level_changed
events.description: Alert owner when risk level increases

events.name: mitigation:overdue
events.description: Notify assignee when action becomes overdue

flows.name: Identify Risk
flows.trigger: User fills risk form and clicks Save
steps: If risk_level = critical → immediately notify risk managers

flows.name: Review Risk
flows.trigger: Risk review_date reached → cron reminder sent
steps: Send reminder email to risk owner
Owner opens risk, reviews current state
Owner can update likelihood, impact, status
Update risk.review_date to next review date

flows.name: Escalate Risk
steps: Open EscalationModal for reason and stakeholder selection
Send escalation notifications to selected stakeholders

computed_fields.name: likelihood_score
computed_fields.formula: MAP likelihood → {rare:1, unlikely:2, possible:3, likely:4, almost_certain:5}

computed_fields.name: impact_score
computed_fields.formula: MAP impact → {negligible:1, minor:2, moderate:3, major:4, severe:5}

computed_fields.name: risk_level
computed_fields.formula: CASE WHEN risk_score<=4 THEN low WHEN risk_score<=9 THEN medium WHEN risk_score<=16 THEN high ELSE critical END

computed_fields.name: overdue_count
computed_fields.formula: COUNT risks WHERE due_date < TODAY AND status NOT IN ('closed','accepted')

domain_rules: Critical and high risks require owner assignment before saving
Closed risks are read-only; require admin to reopen
Risk reviews must be logged at least as frequently as review_date interval
Escalated risks trigger immediate notifications to risk_manager role users
Mitigation action types: avoid (remove risk source), reduce (lower likelihood/impact), transfer (insurance/contract), accept (document acceptance), contingency (response plan if risk occurs)
Soft delete preserves risk audit history; risk_id sequence never reused
generation_rules: Risk heatmap is a 5x5 matrix grid rendered with CSS grid; each cell is a button showing risk count
likelihood_score and impact_score stored in DB for performance; recalculated on enum change
Risk ID auto-increments per org starting from RSK-0001
Review reminders sent via server cron 7 days before review_date
Overdue mitigation actions highlighted in red; overdue detection runs daily
Soft delete only — filter deleted_at IS NULL on all queries
Risk exports include all fields + mitigation actions in a single CSV/PDF
Heatmap color gradient: green (low-left) to red (high-right), matching ISO 31000 standard

org_id_injection.middleware: req.body.org_id is stripped; injected from req.user.org_id

conflict_response.message: Record was modified by another user. Refresh and retry.

concurrency.high_contention_tables: see entities with high write concurrency

audit_trail.triggers: application-level (before write hook) — never DB trigger to avoid log bloat

standard_constraints.name: chk_{table}_deleted_order
standard_constraints.expression: deleted_at IS NULL OR deleted_at >= created_at

db_constraints_policy.enum_validation: DB ENUM types preferred over VARCHAR + CHECK; enum values defined in global enums registry

soft_delete_policy.filter: deleted_at IS NULL applied globally via query builder middleware
soft_delete_policy.cascade: Parent delete soft-deletes children via application cascade, not DB cascade
soft_delete_policy.hard_delete: Prohibited in production; available only via admin CLI with audit log
soft_delete_policy.restore: Set deleted_at = NULL; triggers audit log entry

naming_conventions.date_fields: Always use _at suffix for TIMESTAMPTZ, _date suffix for DATE
naming_conventions.id_fields: Primary key is always id; foreign keys are {entity}_id
